Tina Chin

Martial Arts Biography

Tina Chin began studying Tai Ji in 1995 under Shi Fu Alex Feng, a renown Traditional Chinese Medicine physician and martial artist in the San Francisco Bay area, at his school, Wu Tao Kuan. Her training from Feng Shi Fu includes forms, applications, push hands, Qi Gong, Taoist studies and meditation, and Wu Chien Pai, a martial art style created by Feng Shi Fu. She has traveled to Shanghai twice to receive intensive instruction in Tai Ji sword, Wu Shu nine-sectional whip, and martial art Qi Gong from Professors at the Shanghai Institute of Physical Culture: Zeng Meiying, Wang San, and Xie Yelei, respectively. She also studied Tai Ji at the Beijing Physical Culture University with Professor Li Qiao Ling. She learned Wu Shu spear and straight sword routines from Shenan Tan. This past summer, she took private Tai Ji barehand and sword lessons for one week with Professor Wang Pei Kun.

In 2000, Tina began Chinese acrobatic training to complement her Wu Shu skills. For Chinese Acrobatics, Tina trains at the Circus Center in San Francisco under Masters Lu Yi and Xia Kemin. Additionally, she takes private acrobatic lessons from Weng Xiao Hong. Lu Yi is an internationally acclaimed former director and star performer of the Nanjing Acrobatic Troupe and currently is the artistic director of the New Pickle Circus and the San Francisco Circus Center. Xia Kemin and Weng Xiao Hong are also former members of the Nanjing Acrobatic Troupe and combined have over sixty years of acrobatic teaching and international performing experience.

Tina is Assistant Instructor at Wu Tao Kuan Institute of Martial Arts in Oakland, California. She gives private Tai Ji basics and form lessons. She is also an active competitor in national and international Wu Shu tournaments, consistently winning medals in Tai Ji barehand and weapon divisions. In 2001, she won the Women’s Grand Champion title in Internal Style at the USA Wu Shu Kung Fu Nationals in San Diego.
